refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of: (1) the measurement boundary (what constitutes 'available'), (2) the evaluation period (e.g., 30 days, 90 days, 1 year), (3) exclusions (planned maintenance windows, customer-initiated pauses), and (4) the role of safety-driven shutdowns. High safety criticality demands that availability not be achieved at the expense of safety; therefore, safety-driven unavailability is acceptable and must be excluded from penalty calculations.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry collection from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; automated logging of uptime/downtime events with root-cause classification (planned maintenance, unplanned failure, safety shutdown, operator pause); monthly/quarterly availability reports with confidence intervals; customer sign-off on evaluation methodology and period.
sub_frs: FR-AVAIL-001: The system shall detect and log all state transitions (available → unavailable, unavailable → available) with timestamp and root cause.
FR-AVAIL-002: The system shall distinguish between planned maintenance downtime, unplanned failure downtime, and safety-driven downtime.
FR-AVAIL-003: The system shall provide real-time and historical availability metrics accessible to Site Manager and System Engineer.
FR-AVAIL-004: The system shall implement redundancy and fault-tolerance mechanisms to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-AVAIL-005: The system shall support rapid diagnosis and recovery procedures to reduce mean time to repair (MTTR).

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized availability monitoring and reporting service that aggregates telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, classifies downtime events, and computes availability KPI in real-time.
DP-B: Distribute availability monitoring logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, with each module self-reporting uptime/downtime; aggregate at the NGHE Solution level.
DP-C: Implement redundant Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems with automatic failover to ensure continuous operation; monitor availability of the redundant pair as a single logical unit.
DP-D: Implement a hybrid approach: centralized monitoring service (DP-A) combined with redundancy at critical subsystems (DP-C) to achieve both observability and fault tolerance.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement a hybrid availability assurance strategy combining (1) a centralized Availability Monitoring Service that aggregates Telemetry and SafetyAlert feeds from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, classifies downtime events (planned, unplanned, safety-driven), and computes real-time availability KPI; and (2) redundancy and fault-tolerance mechanisms at the Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystem levels to minimize unplanned downtime, with automatic failover and health-check protocols.

potential_sources: Coupling between Availability Monitoring Service and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces is well-defined and unidirectional (monitoring service consumes, modules produce).
Coupling between redundancy logic and failover mechanisms is internal to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; no cross-module coupling.
Coupling between availability KPI computation and customer reporting is mediated by a well-defined metrics interface (not yet named, but feasible).
coupling_check.notes: DP-D avoids tight coupling by separating concerns: monitoring (observability) is decoupled from redundancy (fault tolerance). Both are necessary for high availability. No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces are reliable and have sufficient bandwidth to support real-time availability monitoring without introducing latency that masks true downtime.
Risk: If Telemetry or SafetyAlert interfaces themselves fail, the Availability Monitoring Service may report false availability. Mitigation: implement watchdog timers and timeout detection.
Assumption: Redundancy at Vehicle and Teleoperation levels is technically and economically feasible (e.g., dual motors, dual communication links, dual compute nodes).
Risk: Redundancy increases system complexity, cost, and power consumption. Mitigation: conduct trade study to identify critical redundancy points.
Assumption: Downtime classification logic (planned vs. unplanned vs. safety-driven) can be automated reliably.
Risk: Misclassification of downtime events could skew availability metrics. Mitigation: implement human review and audit trails for edge cases.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but should be refined to explicitly specify: (1) the redundancy strategy (active-active, active-passive, N+1), (2) the failover detection and switching time (RTO/RPO targets), (3) the telemetry sampling rate and latency budget for availability monitoring, and (4) the downtime classification decision tree (rules for distinguishing planned, unplanned, and safety-driven events). These refinements will be addressed in the sub-FR decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. It is responsible for: (1) orchestrating the Availability Monitoring Service, (2) aggregating availability metrics from all subsystems, (3) enforcing availability targets and SLAs, (4) coordinating redundancy and failover across subsystems, and (5) exposing availability KPI to external stakeholders (Site Manager, System Engineer, Project Manager).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingests real-time operational data (uptime/downtime events, state transitions, health status) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Used by Availability Monitoring Service to compute availability metrics.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Ingests safety-driven shutdown signals and safety status from Safety Services module. Used to classify downtime as safety-driven and exclude from availability penalty calculations.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational tasks and scheduling information from Site Manager. Used to distinguish between planned maintenance windows (excluded from availability calculation) and operational periods (included).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides communication backbone for Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Tasking interfaces. Critical for real-time availability monitoring; Network Link failures directly impact availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for availability monitoring and KPI management. Availability is a system-level property that requires integration across all subsystems. Minimal diffusion: only the Availability Monitoring Service logic and KPI computation are contained here; redundancy logic remains in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

Vehicle module could host redundancy and fault-tolerance mechanisms for its own subsystems (e.g., dual motors, dual sensors), but availability monitoring and KPI computation are system-level concerns that should not be duplicated in Vehicle. Assigning availability monitoring to Vehicle would create diffusion and coupling.

Similar to Vehicle: Teleoperation could implement its own redundancy (e.g., dual communication links, dual control channels), but system-level availability monitoring should not be distributed to Teleoperation. High diffusion risk if availability logic is split across modules.

Safety Services is responsible for safety-driven shutdowns and alerts (SafetyAlert interface), not for availability monitoring. Assigning availability KPI management to Safety Services would violate separation of concerns and create high diffusion.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System Engineering team or dedicated Availability/Reliability Engineering team)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Availability Monitoring Service and KPI computation logic are best contained within the NGHE Solution module because: (1) availability is a system-level property that requires integration across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) centralizing monitoring avoids diffusion and duplication; (3) NGHE Solution is the natural integration point for Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Tasking interfaces; (4) ownership and accountability for availability targets are clearest at the system level. Redundancy and fault-tolerance mechanisms remain distributed in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, where they are most effective.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a bottleneck if Availability Monitoring Service is not designed for scalability and low latency. Mitigation: implement asynchronous event processing and caching.

Risk: Dependency on Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Tasking interfaces introduces external coupling. If these interfaces are unreliable or have high latency, availability monitoring accuracy suffers. Mitigation: implement watchdog timers, timeout detection, and graceful degradation.

Risk: Availability KPI computation logic may be complex (e.g., handling edge cases in downtime classification). Complexity could lead to bugs or misclassification. Mitigation: implement comprehensive unit tests, integration tests, and human audit trails.

Risk: NGHE Solution module may grow large if it also contains other system-level services (e.g., logging, configuration management). Mitigation: enforce clear module boundaries and consider splitting into sub-modules if needed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: The assignment of Availability Monitoring Service to NGHE Solution is sound. However, the IM boundary should be refined to explicitly define: (1) the internal structure of NGHE Solution (e.g., Availability Monitoring Service as a sub-component), (2) the interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services modules (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link), (3) the ownership and governance model (who is responsible for availability targets, who manages redundancy trade-offs), and (4) the deployment and integration strategy (how Availability Monitoring Service is deployed, how it integrates with existing systems). These refinements will be addressed in the sub-FR decomposition and detailed design phases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid availability assurance strategy) directly addresses FR-AVAIL (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP specifies both observability (Availability Monitoring Service) and fault tolerance (redundancy and failover), which are necessary and sufficient to meet the availability target. The DP is implementable and testable. No fundamental mismatch between FR and DP.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: Availability Monitoring Service is centralized in NGHE Solution; no duplication across modules.
Redundancy logic remains distributed in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, which is appropriate for fault tolerance.
Potential diffusion if availability monitoring logic leaks into Vehicle or Teleoperation modules. Mitigation: enforce clear module boundaries and code review.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct container for system-level availability monitoring and KPI management. Containment score of 0.95 indicates strong fit. External interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are well-defined and necessary for availability monitoring. No artificial module boundaries or missing interfaces detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-AVAIL is feasible through: (1) continuous telemetry collection and logging, (2) automated availability metric computation, (3) monthly/quarterly availability reports, (4) customer sign-off on evaluation methodology. Verification requires instrumentation of Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to report uptime/downtime events, which is a reasonable requirement.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-AVAIL to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-AVAIL-001 through FR-AVAIL-005) are allocated to specific modules: FR-AVAIL-001 and FR-AVAIL-002 to Vehicle and Teleoperation (event logging); FR-AVAIL-003 to NGHE Solution (metrics reporting); FR-AVAIL-004 to Vehicle and Teleoperation (redundancy); FR-AVAIL-005 to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services (diagnosis and recovery). No ambiguity in allocation.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-D to specify redundancy strategy (active-active, active-passive, N+1) and failover detection/switching time (RTO/RPO targets).
Define telemetry sampling rate and latency budget for availability monitoring to ensure real-time accuracy.
Develop downtime classification decision tree (rules for distinguishing planned, unplanned, and safety-driven events) to reduce misclassification risk.
Specify the internal structure of NGHE Solution module (e.g., Availability Monitoring Service as a sub-component) and its interfaces with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
Define ownership and governance model for availability targets and redundancy trade-offs.
Conduct trade study on redundancy costs (complexity, power, weight) vs. availability benefits to validate DP-D feasibility.

decision.justification: FR-AVAIL is a high-level KPI that requires decomposition into lower-level functional requirements (FR-AVAIL-001 through FR-AVAIL-005) to be implementable. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate. However, the sub-FRs must be further decomposed to define specific design parameters for: (1) event logging and classification (FR-AVAIL-001, FR-AVAIL-002), (2) metrics reporting and dashboards (FR-AVAIL-003), (3) redundancy and failover mechanisms (FR-AVAIL-004), and (4) diagnosis and recovery procedures (FR-AVAIL-005). Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to make the design concrete and implementable.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-AVAIL-001: The system shall detect and log all state transitions (available → unavailable, unavailable → available) with timestamp and root cause.
FR-AVAIL-002: The system shall distinguish between planned maintenance downtime, unplanned failure downtime, and safety-driven downtime.
FR-AVAIL-003: The system shall provide real-time and historical availability metrics accessible to Site Manager and System Engineer.
FR-AVAIL-004: The system shall implement redundancy and fault-tolerance mechanisms to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-AVAIL-005: The system shall support rapid diagnosis and recovery procedures to reduce mean time to repair (MTTR).

assumptions: Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces are reliable and have sufficient bandwidth for real-time availability monitoring.
Redundancy at Vehicle and Teleoperation levels is technically and economically feasible.
Downtime classification logic can be automated reliably with acceptable false-positive/false-negative rates.
Evaluation period for availability measurement is agreed with customer (e.g., 30 days, 90 days, 1 year).
Planned maintenance windows are known in advance and can be excluded from availability calculation.
Safety-driven shutdowns are acceptable and should be excluded from availability penalty calculations.
open_risks: Telemetry or SafetyAlert interface failures could cause false availability reporting. Mitigation: implement watchdog timers and timeout detection.
Redundancy increases system complexity, cost, and power consumption. Mitigation: conduct trade study to identify critical redundancy points.
Downtime classification misclassification could skew availability metrics. Mitigation: implement human review and audit trails.
NGHE Solution module may become a bottleneck if Availability Monitoring Service is not designed for scalability. Mitigation: implement asynchronous event processing and caching.
Network Link failures directly impact availability monitoring and failover. Mitigation: implement redundant communication paths and graceful degradation.
evidence_needed: Customer agreement on availability target (≥0.97 or alternative), evaluation period, and exclusions (planned maintenance, safety-driven shutdowns).
Trade study on redundancy strategy (active-active, active-passive, N+1) and cost-benefit analysis.
Telemetry and SafetyAlert interface specifications (bandwidth, latency, reliability requirements).
Downtime classification decision tree and validation data (historical downtime events with root causes).
Availability monitoring service design and performance analysis (latency, throughput, scalability).
Test plan for availability KPI verification (continuous monitoring, monthly/quarterly reports, customer sign-off).
